info.description: The Amazon Shipping API is designed to support outbound shipping use cases both for orders originating on Amazon-owned marketplaces as well as external channels/marketplaces. With these APIs, you can request shipping rates, create shipments, cancel shipments, and track shipments.
info.title: Amazon Shipping API

get.description:
Returns a list of access points in proximity of input postal code.

**Usage Plan:**

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 80 | 100 |

The `x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit` response header returns the usage plan rate limits that were applied to the requested operation, when available. The table above indicates the default rate and burst values for this operation. Selling partners whose business demands require higher throughput may see higher rate and burst values then those shown here. For more information, see [Usage Plans and Rate Limits in the Selling Partner API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/usage-plans-and-rate-limits-in-the-sp-api).

x-docgen-enum-table-extension.description: Counter, aka Helix, is an assisted access point where customers interact with a store associate to collect or return packages. Counter stores do not have Locker
It denotes a physical Locker present at Locker+ stores
Omni lockers are similar to core lockers installed at apartment buildings. Core and Omni will have common base hardware platform

Odin lockers rely on Bluetooth connection as there is no screen or scanner on the locker. Drivers, customers, and even technicians can interact with the locker using an app (different depending on the user).
Dobby lockers are Odin lockers installed at apartment complexes and available only to residents.

Core lockers are the traditional and initial style of lockers when Hub was launched. Core lockers have a screen, keypad and scanner.  Customer either enter their pickup code using the Locker screen or scan pickup code barcode to pick up their package.

Networks that are owned end-to-end by commercial carriers (CC - e.g. DHL, UPS, DPD and Postal organizations), who operate both the transportation leg and the access point aspect. These networks offer a range of pickup points including: 3P Lockers, legacy Postal Offices, 3P Counters (e.g. Gas Stations and Retail Outlets - supermarkets, bookstores, news-stands etc).

Locker+, formerly known as Campus, are attended, physical stores, where the Locker is used dynamically by the staff to keep packages ready for customer pickup. Locker+ store can have multiple Lockers installed,

parameters.name: x-amzn-shipping-business-id
parameters.description: Amazon shipping business to assume for this request. The default is AmazonShipping_UK.

x-docgen-enum-table-extension.description: The United States Amazon shipping business.
The India Amazon shipping business.
The United Kingdom Amazon shipping business.
The United Arab Emirates Amazon shipping business.
The Saudi Arabia Amazon shipping business.
The Egypt Amazon shipping business.
The Italy Amazon shipping business.
The Spain Amazon shipping business.
The France Amazon shipping business.
The Japan Amazon shipping business.

400.description: Request has missing or invalid parameters and cannot be parsed.

errors.message: Request is missing or has invalid parameters
errors.details: Access Point types cannot be null

x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit.description: Your rate limit (requests per second) for this operation.

401.description: The request's Authorization header is not formatted correctly or does not contain a valid token.

errors.message: You don't have access to the requested response or the credentials are invalid.

403.description: Indicates that access to the resource is forbidden. Possible reasons include Access Denied, Unauthorized, Expired Token, or Invalid Signature.

errors.message: You don't have access to the requested response or the credentials are invalid.

404.description: The resource specified does not exist.

errors.message: The requested resource is invalid or doesn't exist

x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit.description: Your rate limit (requests per second) for this operation.

413.description: The request size exceeded the maximum accepted size.

errors.message: The request payload size is greater than maximum accepted size.

415.description: The request payload is in an unsupported format.

errors.message: The request payload format is not supported.

429.description: The frequency of requests was greater than allowed.

errors.message: The total number of requests exceeded your allowed limit.

500.description: An unexpected condition occurred that prevented the server from fulfilling the request.

errors.message: Something went wrong while processing the request.

503.description: Temporary overloading or maintenance of the server.

errors.message: Service temporarily unavailable or down for maintenance. Please try again after sometime.

AccessPointsMap.description: Map of type of access point to list of access points

DayOfWeekTimeMap.description: Map of day of the week to operating hours of that day

ExceptionOperatingHours.description: Defines exceptions to standard operating hours for certain date ranges.

OperatingHours.description: The hours in which the access point shall remain operational

DateRange.description: Date Range for query the results.

CountryCode.description: The two digit country code. Follows ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

GetAccessPointsResponse.description: The response schema for the GetAccessPoints operation.

AccessPointList.description: List of relevant Access points requested by shipper. These access points are sorted by proximity to postal code, and are limited to 40. We have internally defined a radius value to render relevant results.

GetAccessPointsResult.description: The payload for the GetAccessPoints API.

City.description: The city or town where the person, business or institution is located.

AccessPointType.description: The type of access point, like counter (HELIX), lockers, etc.

x-docgen-enum-table-extension.description: Counter, aka Helix, is an assisted access point where customers interact with a store associate to collect or return packages. Counter stores do not have Locker
It denotes a physical Locker present at Locker+ stores
Omni lockers are similar to core lockers installed at apartment buildings. Core and Omni will have common base hardware platform

ErrorList.description: A list of error responses returned when a request is unsuccessful.

TimeOfDay.description: Denotes time of the day, used for defining opening or closing time of access points

hourOfDay.description: Denotes hour of the day, used for defining opening or closing time of access points

minuteOfHour.description: Denotes minute of the hour, used for defining opening or closing time of access points

secondOfMinute.description: Denotes second of the minute, used for defining opening or closing time of access points

code.description: An error code that identifies the type of error that occurred.

message.description: A message that describes the error condition.

details.description: Additional details that can help the caller understand or fix the issue.

Error.description: Error response returned when the request is unsuccessful.

AccessibilityAttributes.description: Defines the accessibility details of the access point.

distance.description: The approximate distance of access point from input postalCode's centroid.

driveTime.description: The approximate (static) drive time from input postal code's centroid.

PostalCode.description: The postal code of that address. It contains a series of letters or digits or both, sometimes including spaces or punctuation.

name.description: Name of entity (store/hub etc) where this access point is located

exceptionOperatingHours.description: Exception operating hours for Access Point

assistanceType.description: Assistance type enum for Access point i.e. STAFF_ASSISTED or SELF_ASSISTED

score.description: The score of access point, based on proximity to postal code and sorting preference. This can be used to sort access point results on shipper's end.

StateOrRegion.description: The state, county or region where the person, business or institution is located.

AccessPointId.description: Unique identifier for the access point

name.description: The name of the person, business or institution at the address.

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address.

companyName.description: The name of the business or institution associated with the address.

email.description: The email address of the contact associated with the address.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the person, business or institution located at that address, including the country calling code.

Geocode.description: Defines the latitude and longitude of the access point.
